arts.ttl: Pedro Neto thriving in new striker position with Chelsea [video]
arts.sum: Chelsea 4-0 Southampton Few would have predicted Pedro Neto to state a case for Chelsea’s striker position this season, but a recent makeshift role for the Portuguese is yielding positive results with the Blues. Neto scored and provided an assist on Tuesday evening as Chelsea crushed Southampton, on what was his second outing as the spearhead of Enzo Maresca’s attack. Having registered an assist against Aston Villa at the weekend, Neto has one goal and two assists in his new role. The former Braga man is as traditional a winger as you could expect to find in the modern game, but the decision to move Neto centrally was provoked by the absence of Senegal international Nicolas Jackson and struggles of others to convince Maresca of their suitability. Neto scored Chelsea’s second, finishing with a crisp left-footed strike having been found by Christopher Nkunku. The Seleção man then turned provider, sending a free-kick into the box which was nodded home by Levi Colwill, making it the first time Neto has scored and assisted in the same game since arriving in the Premier League. Brilliant Neto! 😤#CFC | #CHESOU pic.twitter.com/vsXDHRAHeW — Chelsea FC (@ChelseaFC) February 26, 2025 "I don't have to run as much" Neto was praised by pundits after the match, not just for his goal but the quality of his movement, especially considering his inexperience in the position. “To be honest [I’m enjoying it] a lot because I don’t have to run as much!” he joked in his post-match interview. “Without running back I have more energy to run forward. “I have a free role there, I like to receive it to feet, like to go in behind, so I’m really enjoying playing there. I want to help the team, that is the most important thing. “When I arrived here [in England] at Wolves, I knew that teams would normally play a high block against us. When I arrived here [at Chelsea] we more faced the low block. In the beginning of this season, teams saw that it was really difficult to press high against us and I was adding to my game playing in small spaces, dribbles in small spaces, these combinations we have. “That’s really important when teams are in a low block. It was a thing I wanted to bring here. Before I used to say that I run a lot in really big spaces and I still have that. But in small spaces, the contact with the ball in the box, to be more calm to put the ball in the net. It’s something I want to add and I will keep improving in that. “In Italy when I was 17 years old, we used to play two strikers and I was playing behind the striker. I always learned that against big guys whether to have the contact first or use movement. I like to play with the contact first, take a step and try to turn. And sometimes I like to give the pass and run in behind. I really enjoy it, so it’s a thing I like and I want to improve it.” By @SeanGillen9

arts.ttl: Former Chelsea director takes pay cut at Man United after Ineos announcement
arts.sum: And after a short interim spell at United, his position has reportedly been made permanent by Ineos, who run football operations at the Red Devils. It comes after several cost-cutting measures were announced by the club on Monday in what United described as a "transformation plan".

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca reveals exactly what he told Cole Palmer on the pitch as Chelsea star endures frustrating night despite Blues beating Southampton 4-0
arts.sum: Cole Palmer had chances to add a goal or two of his own on Tuesday but could not find his mark and Enzo Maresca was spotted in deep discussion with Palmer on the pitch after Chelsea's latest win.

arts.ttl: Is Palmer's form a worry for Chelsea? No goal from SEVEN attempts vs sorry Saints
arts.sum: Cole Palmer's goalless run stretched to seven games after failing to find the net in Chelsea's 4-0 win over Southampton despite having seven efforts on goal during the game.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca urges Chelsea fans to show trust after BlueCo protest
arts.sum: Tuesday’s 4-0 win over Southampton was preceded by a demonstration against the club’s owners.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth aware of Kepa Arrizabalaga 'difficulty' as Cherries identify two replacements
arts.sum: Bournemouth are reportedly considering two goalkeeper targets amid uncertainty surrounding the future of Chelsea loanee Kepa Arrizabalaga.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca sends message to Chelsea fans after protest ahead of big win over Southampton
arts.sum: Around 200 fans gathered ahead of the match to protest against the Chelsea owners
How we rated the players. The post Chelsea v Southampton player ratings appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Chelsea ‘cannot rely on Cole Palmer for every game’ as dry spell continues
arts.sum: Chelsea boss Enzo Maresca says the club cannot expect Cole Palmer to score every week after the star man drew a blank against Southampton on Tuesday evening. The 22-year-old took seven shots, more than any of his teammates, and spurned a number of good chances as the Blues ran out 4-0 winners at Stamford Bridge.
